summary: Jeff Atwood recommends the Wintellect ASP.NET FAQ as a valuable resource for ASP.NET developers. Despite being somewhat dated, he finds the FAQ contains genuinely insightful material covering both basic and advanced topics. The post highlights the value of well-constructed FAQs built from real-world questions.
key_points: Well-constructed FAQs based on real-world questions are invaluable resources
The Wintellect FAQ covers both basic and advanced ASP.NET topics
Good documentation ranges from mundane syntax questions to complex threading concerns
money_quotes: Nothing beats a well constructed FAQ based on lots of real-world questions.
It's a mixture of the mundane and the sublime.
Guaranteed to contain a few things you didn't know you needed to know about ASP.NET.
key_insight: The best technical FAQs are those built from accumulated real-world questions, covering everything from basic syntax to complex architectural concerns.
